html:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8">
<style>
html, body { margin:0; padding:0; width:100%; height:100%; overflow:hidden; background:#fff; }
svg { width:100%; height:100%; display:block; background:#fff; }
text { font-family:"Times New Roman", Times, serif; fill:#000; dominant-baseline:middle; text-anchor:middle; }
.formulaText { font-weight:bold; font-size:15; }
.tickText { font-size:16; }
.axisLabel { font-size:17; font-weight:bold; }
.edge { stroke:#000; stroke-width:0.8; stroke-dasharray:6 7; fill:none; }
.axis { stroke:#000; stroke-width:0.8; fill:none; }
.node { fill:#000; stroke:#000; stroke-width:0.8; }
.outTri { fill:#000; stroke:#000; stroke-width:0.8; }
.box { fill:#fff; stroke:#000; stroke-width:0.8; }
.hoverable { cursor:pointer; }
</style>
</head>
<body>
<svg id="fig" viewBox="0 0 600 539" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<defs>
<marker id="axisEnd" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" refX="5" refY="3" orient="auto" markerUnits="strokeWidth">
<path d="M0,0 L0,6 L6,3 z" fill="#000"></path>
</marker>
<marker id="axisStart" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" refX="1" refY="3" orient="auto" markerUnits="strokeWidth">
<path d="M6,0 L6,6 L0,3 z" fill="#000"></path>
</marker>
<marker id="arr" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" refX="5" refY="3" orient="auto" markerUnits="strokeWidth">
<path d="M0,0 L0,6 L6,3 z" fill="#000"></path>
</marker>
</defs>

<g id="panel-scale" class="panel hoverable" data-title="Scaling map" data-body="This panel shows the linear transformation x_out = 2x_in. Inputs between −0.5 and 0.5 are stretched so their outputs occupy the full interval from −1 to 1." data-tip="x_out = 2x_in: expands input values">
<rect class="box hoverable" x="9" y="129" width="87" height="25" data-title="Scaling equation" data-body="The equation doubles each input value. A small input interval is mapped to a wider output interval, so neighboring input samples spread apart." data-tip="Scaling equation: doubles the input"></rect>
<text class="formulaText" x="52.5" y="141.5" data-tip="Scaling equation: doubles the input">x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="10">out</tspan> =2x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="10">in</tspan></text>

<line class="axis hoverable" x1="82" y1="210" x2="219" y2="210" marker-start="url(#axisStart)" marker-end="url(#axisEnd)" data-title="Input axis" data-body="The lower number line is the input coordinate x_in. The black dots indicate the sampled input values before the transformation is applied." data-tip="Input axis: values before mapping"></line>
<line class="axis hoverable" x1="84" y1="64" x2="219" y2="64" marker-end="url(#axisEnd)" data-title="Output axis" data-body="The upper number line is the output coordinate x_out. Arrowheads at this axis show where each input sample lands after the transformation." data-tip="Output axis: values after mapping"></line>

<text class="axisLabel" x="150" y="26">x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="11">out</tspan></text>
<text class="axisLabel" x="150" y="246">x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="11">in</tspan></text>
<text class="tickText" x="94" y="50">−1</text><text class="tickText" x="122" y="50">−0.5</text><text class="tickText" x="150" y="50">0</text><text class="tickText" x="178" y="50">0.5</text><text class="tickText" x="206" y="50">1</text>
<text class="tickText" x="94" y="226">−1</text><text class="tickText" x="122" y="226">−0.5</text><text class="tickText" x="150" y="226">0</text><text class="tickText" x="178" y="226">0.5</text><text class="tickText" x="206" y="226">1</text>

<line id="s-e1" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="122" y1="205.9" x2="94" y2="66.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sample mapping" data-body="This arrow carries x_in = −0.5 to x_out = −1. Doubling a negative input moves it farther left on the output axis." data-tip="−0.5 maps to −1"></line>
<line id="s-e2" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="136" y1="205.9" x2="122" y2="66.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sample mapping" data-body="This arrow carries x_in = −0.25 to x_out = −0.5. The output is twice as far from zero as the input." data-tip="−0.25 maps to −0.5"></line>
<line id="s-e3" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="150" y1="205.9" x2="150" y2="66.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sample mapping" data-body="The zero input maps to zero output. Linear scaling keeps the origin fixed." data-tip="0 maps to 0"></line>
<line id="s-e4" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="164" y1="205.9" x2="178" y2="66.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sample mapping" data-body="This arrow carries x_in = 0.25 to x_out = 0.5. Positive values are pushed farther to the right." data-tip="0.25 maps to 0.5"></line>
<line id="s-e5" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="178" y1="205.9" x2="206" y2="66.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sample mapping" data-body="This arrow carries x_in = 0.5 to x_out = 1. The transformed samples span the full output range shown." data-tip="0.5 maps to 1"></line>

<circle id="s-n1" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="122" cy="210" r="3.5" data-title="Input sample" data-body="This black dot is one sampled input value. The dashed arrow above it shows the output produced by the function." data-tip="Input sample −0.5"></circle>
<circle id="s-n2" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="136" cy="210" r="3.5" data-title="Input sample" data-body="This black dot is one sampled input value. The transformation sends it to the corresponding arrowhead on the output axis." data-tip="Input sample −0.25"></circle>
<circle id="s-n3" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="150" cy="210" r="3.5" data-title="Input sample" data-body="This input sample lies at zero. Because the function is linear through the origin, its output also lies at zero." data-tip="Input sample 0"></circle>
<circle id="s-n4" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="164" cy="210" r="3.5" data-title="Input sample" data-body="This input sample is positive. Doubling moves its output farther to the right." data-tip="Input sample 0.25"></circle>
<circle id="s-n5" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="178" cy="210" r="3.5" data-title="Input sample" data-body="This is the largest input sample shown in the active set. Under x_out = 2x_in it reaches the right edge of the output range." data-tip="Input sample 0.5"></circle>
<polygon id="s-t1" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="90.5,69.5 97.5,69.5 94,63" data-tip="Output −1"></polygon>
<polygon id="s-t2" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="118.5,69.5 125.5,69.5 122,63" data-tip="Output −0.5"></polygon>
<polygon id="s-t3" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="146.5,69.5 153.5,69.5 150,63" data-tip="Output 0"></polygon>
<polygon id="s-t4" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="174.5,69.5 181.5,69.5 178,63" data-tip="Output 0.5"></polygon>
<polygon id="s-t5" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="202.5,69.5 209.5,69.5 206,63" data-tip="Output 1"></polygon>
</g>

<g id="panel-shift" class="panel hoverable" data-title="Shift-and-scale map" data-body="This panel shows x_out = (x_in + 1)/2. The input interval from −1 to 1 is shifted and compressed so that it lands from 0 to 1." data-tip="(x_in + 1)/2: shifts and compresses">
<rect class="box hoverable" x="345" y="129" width="89" height="32" data-title="Shift-and-scale equation" data-body="Adding 1 shifts the input interval rightward, and dividing by 2 compresses it. Together these operations map −1 to 0 and 1 to 1." data-tip="Affine equation: shift then halve"></rect>
<text class="formulaText" x="389.5" y="143">x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="10">out</tspan> = <tspan font-size="13">x</tspan><tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="9">in</tspan><tspan font-size="13">+1</tspan></text>
<line x1="395" y1="149" x2="424" y2="149" stroke="#000" stroke-width="0.8"></line>
<text class="formulaText" x="409.5" y="155" font-size="12">2</text>

<line class="axis hoverable" x1="419" y1="210" x2="556" y2="210" marker-start="url(#axisStart)" marker-end="url(#axisEnd)" data-title="Input axis" data-body="The lower axis shows the original inputs from −1 to 1. Each black dot is mapped upward to the output axis." data-tip="Input axis: −1 to 1"></line>
<line class="axis hoverable" x1="421" y1="64" x2="556" y2="64" marker-end="url(#axisEnd)" data-title="Output axis" data-body="The upper axis shows the transformed outputs. Here the samples occupy only the right half of the displayed output scale, from 0 to 1." data-tip="Output axis: 0 to 1"></line>

<text class="axisLabel" x="489" y="26">x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="11">out</tspan></text>
<text class="axisLabel" x="489" y="246">x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="11">in</tspan></text>
<text class="tickText" x="432" y="50">−1</text><text class="tickText" x="461" y="50">−0.5</text><text class="tickText" x="489" y="50">0</text><text class="tickText" x="518" y="50">0.5</text><text class="tickText" x="546" y="50">1</text>
<text class="tickText" x="432" y="226">−1</text><text class="tickText" x="461" y="226">−0.5</text><text class="tickText" x="489" y="226">0</text><text class="tickText" x="518" y="226">0.5</text><text class="tickText" x="546" y="226">1</text>

<line id="a-e1" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="432" y1="205.9" x2="489" y2="66.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sample mapping" data-body="The leftmost input, x_in = −1, maps to x_out = 0. The affine transformation moves the interval into the nonnegative half." data-tip="−1 maps to 0"></line>
<line id="a-e2" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="461" y1="205.9" x2="503.5" y2="66.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sample mapping" data-body="This sample maps to one quarter on the output scale. The division by 2 reduces distances between neighboring output samples." data-tip="−0.5 maps to 0.25"></line>
<line id="a-e3" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="489" y1="205.9" x2="518" y2="66.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sample mapping" data-body="The central input x_in = 0 maps to x_out = 0.5. The output interval is centered at one half." data-tip="0 maps to 0.5"></line>
<line id="a-e4" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="518" y1="205.9" x2="532" y2="66.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sample mapping" data-body="This positive input maps to three quarters. Equal input spacing remains equal after this linear affine map." data-tip="0.5 maps to 0.75"></line>
<line id="a-e5" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="546" y1="205.9" x2="546" y2="66.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sample mapping" data-body="The rightmost input x_in = 1 maps to x_out = 1. This fixes the upper endpoint of the transformed interval." data-tip="1 maps to 1"></line>

<circle id="a-n1" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="432" cy="210" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample −1"></circle>
<circle id="a-n2" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="461" cy="210" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample −0.5"></circle>
<circle id="a-n3" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="489" cy="210" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample 0"></circle>
<circle id="a-n4" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="518" cy="210" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample 0.5"></circle>
<circle id="a-n5" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="546" cy="210" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample 1"></circle>
<polygon id="a-t1" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="485.5,69.5 492.5,69.5 489,63" data-tip="Output 0"></polygon>
<polygon id="a-t2" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="500,69.5 507,69.5 503.5,63" data-tip="Output 0.25"></polygon>
<polygon id="a-t3" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="514.5,69.5 521.5,69.5 518,63" data-tip="Output 0.5"></polygon>
<polygon id="a-t4" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="528.5,69.5 535.5,69.5 532,63" data-tip="Output 0.75"></polygon>
<polygon id="a-t5" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="542.5,69.5 549.5,69.5 546,63" data-tip="Output 1"></polygon>
</g>

<g id="panel-relu" class="panel hoverable" data-title="ReLU map" data-body="This panel shows x_out = relu(x_in), which clips negative inputs to zero and leaves positive inputs unchanged. Several different negative inputs therefore collapse to the same output." data-tip="ReLU: negatives become zero">
<rect class="box hoverable" x="9" y="390" width="125" height="26" data-title="ReLU equation" data-body="The rectified linear unit is zero for negative inputs and equal to the input for positive inputs. It is a simple nonlinearity that creates a flat region and a linear region." data-tip="ReLU equation: max(0, x)"></rect>
<text class="formulaText" x="71.5" y="403">x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="10">out</tspan> =relu(x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="10">in</tspan>)</text>

<line class="axis hoverable" x1="120" y1="469" x2="257" y2="469" marker-start="url(#axisStart)" marker-end="url(#axisEnd)" data-title="Input axis" data-body="The lower axis gives the input samples. Negative inputs appear to the left of zero and positive inputs to the right." data-tip="Input axis for ReLU"></line>
<line class="axis hoverable" x1="121" y1="330" x2="256" y2="330" marker-end="url(#axisEnd)" data-title="Output axis" data-body="The upper axis gives the ReLU outputs. Notice that all negative inputs are sent to the same output value, zero." data-tip="Output axis for ReLU"></line>

<text class="axisLabel" x="188" y="294">x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="11">out</tspan></text>
<text class="axisLabel" x="188" y="506">x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="11">in</tspan></text>
<text class="tickText" x="131" y="316">−1</text><text class="tickText" x="159" y="316">−0.5</text><text class="tickText" x="188" y="316">0</text><text class="tickText" x="216" y="316">0.5</text><text class="tickText" x="244" y="316">1</text>
<text class="tickText" x="131" y="485">−1</text><text class="tickText" x="159" y="485">−0.5</text><text class="tickText" x="188" y="485">0</text><text class="tickText" x="216" y="485">0.5</text><text class="tickText" x="244" y="485">1</text>

<line id="r-e1" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="131" y1="464.9" x2="188" y2="332.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="ReLU clipping" data-body="The negative input x_in = −1 is clipped to output 0. ReLU discards the sign and magnitude of negative values." data-tip="−1 maps to 0"></line>
<line id="r-e2" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="159" y1="464.9" x2="188" y2="332.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="ReLU clipping" data-body="The negative input x_in = −0.5 also maps to 0. Multiple negative inputs collapse to the same output." data-tip="−0.5 maps to 0"></line>
<line id="r-e3" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="188" y1="464.9" x2="188" y2="332.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="ReLU fixed point" data-body="The input x_in = 0 remains at output 0. This is the hinge point where ReLU changes from flat to linear." data-tip="0 maps to 0"></line>
<line id="r-e4" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="216" y1="464.9" x2="216" y2="332.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Positive ReLU mapping" data-body="For x_in = 0.5, ReLU returns the same value. Positive inputs pass through unchanged." data-tip="0.5 maps to 0.5"></line>
<line id="r-e5" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="244" y1="464.9" x2="244" y2="332.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Positive ReLU mapping" data-body="For x_in = 1, the output is also 1. ReLU is linear on the positive side." data-tip="1 maps to 1"></line>

<circle id="r-n1" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="131" cy="469" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample −1"></circle>
<circle id="r-n2" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="159" cy="469" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample −0.5"></circle>
<circle id="r-n3" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="188" cy="469" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample 0"></circle>
<circle id="r-n4" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="216" cy="469" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample 0.5"></circle>
<circle id="r-n5" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="244" cy="469" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample 1"></circle>
<polygon id="r-t1" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="184.5,335.5 191.5,335.5 188,329" data-tip="Output 0"></polygon>
<polygon id="r-t2" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="185.5,335.5 192.5,335.5 189,329" data-tip="Output 0"></polygon>
<polygon id="r-t3" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="183.5,335.5 190.5,335.5 187,329" data-tip="Output 0"></polygon>
<polygon id="r-t4" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="212.5,335.5 219.5,335.5 216,329" data-tip="Output 0.5"></polygon>
<polygon id="r-t5" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="240.5,335.5 247.5,335.5 244,329" data-tip="Output 1"></polygon>
</g>

<g id="panel-sigmoid" class="panel hoverable" data-title="Sigmoid map" data-body="This panel shows x_out = sigmoid(x_in). Large negative inputs map near 0, large positive inputs map near 1, and inputs around zero change most strongly." data-tip="Sigmoid: squashes values into 0–1">
<rect class="box hoverable" x="284" y="391" width="151" height="26" data-title="Sigmoid equation" data-body="The sigmoid function smoothly squashes any real input into the interval from 0 to 1. It saturates near the endpoints, so very large positive or negative inputs change the output only slightly." data-tip="Sigmoid equation: smooth squashing"></rect>
<text class="formulaText" x="359.5" y="404">x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="10">out</tspan> =sigmoid(x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="10">in</tspan>)</text>

<line class="axis hoverable" x1="419" y1="469" x2="556" y2="469" marker-start="url(#axisStart)" marker-end="url(#axisEnd)" data-title="Input axis" data-body="The lower axis spans a wider input range, from −5 to 5. These large inputs illustrate the saturation of the sigmoid." data-tip="Input axis: −5 to 5"></line>
<line class="axis hoverable" x1="421" y1="331" x2="556" y2="331" marker-end="url(#axisEnd)" data-title="Output axis" data-body="The upper axis shows outputs from roughly 0 to 1. Sigmoid compresses the wide input range into this narrow output interval." data-tip="Output axis: sigmoid range"></line>

<text class="axisLabel" x="489" y="294">x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="11">out</tspan></text>
<text class="axisLabel" x="489" y="506">x<tspan baseline-shift="sub" font-size="11">in</tspan></text>
<text class="tickText" x="432" y="317">−1</text><text class="tickText" x="461" y="317">−0.5</text><text class="tickText" x="489" y="317">0</text><text class="tickText" x="518" y="317">0.5</text><text class="tickText" x="546" y="317">1</text>
<text class="tickText" x="423" y="485">−5</text><text class="tickText" x="461" y="485">−2.5</text><text class="tickText" x="489" y="485">0</text><text class="tickText" x="518" y="485">2.5</text><text class="tickText" x="546" y="485">5</text>

<line id="g-e1" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="432" y1="464.9" x2="490" y2="333.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sigmoid saturation" data-body="A very negative input maps close to 0. In this saturated region, changing the input has little effect on the output." data-tip="−5 maps near 0"></line>
<line id="g-e2" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="461" y1="464.9" x2="493.5" y2="333.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sigmoid saturation" data-body="The input −2.5 also maps to a small positive output. The sigmoid is beginning to rise but is still near its lower saturation region." data-tip="−2.5 maps near 0.08"></line>
<line id="g-e3" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="489" y1="464.9" x2="518" y2="333.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sigmoid midpoint" data-body="The input 0 maps to 0.5. This midpoint is where the sigmoid curve is steepest and most sensitive to input changes." data-tip="0 maps to 0.5"></line>
<line id="g-e4" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="518" y1="464.9" x2="542" y2="333.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sigmoid saturation" data-body="The input 2.5 maps close to 1. As the sigmoid approaches its upper limit, the output changes more slowly." data-tip="2.5 maps near 0.92"></line>
<line id="g-e5" class="edge signal hoverable" x1="546" y1="464.9" x2="546" y2="333.8" marker-end="url(#arr)" data-title="Sigmoid saturation" data-body="A very positive input maps almost exactly to 1. This upper saturation keeps outputs bounded." data-tip="5 maps near 1"></line>

<circle id="g-n1" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="432" cy="469" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample −5"></circle>
<circle id="g-n2" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="461" cy="469" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample −2.5"></circle>
<circle id="g-n3" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="489" cy="469" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample 0"></circle>
<circle id="g-n4" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="518" cy="469" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample 2.5"></circle>
<circle id="g-n5" class="node hoverable signalNode" cx="546" cy="469" r="3.5" data-tip="Input sample 5"></circle>
<polygon id="g-t1" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="486.5,336.5 493.5,336.5 490,330" data-tip="Output near 0"></polygon>
<polygon id="g-t2" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="490,336.5 497,336.5 493.5,330" data-tip="Output near 0.08"></polygon>
<polygon id="g-t3" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="514.5,336.5 521.5,336.5 518,330" data-tip="Output 0.5"></polygon>
<polygon id="g-t4" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="538.5,336.5 545.5,336.5 542,330" data-tip="Output near 0.92"></polygon>
<polygon id="g-t5" class="outTri hoverable signalTri" points="542.5,336.5 549.5,336.5 546,330" data-tip="Output near 1"></polygon>
</g>
</svg>

<div id="tt" style="position:fixed; background:rgba(0,0,0,0.55); color:#fff; font:11px/1.4 sans-serif; padding:3px 8px; border-radius:4px; pointer-events:none; z-index:100; white-space:nowrap; display:none;"></div>

<script>
try {
  const svg = document.getElementById('fig');
  const tt = document.getElementById('tt');
  const original = new Map();
  document.querySelectorAll('.edge,.axis,.node,.outTri,.box').forEach(el => {
    original.set(el, {
      stroke: el.getAttribute('stroke') || getComputedStyle(el).stroke,
      fill: el.getAttribute('fill') || getComputedStyle(el).fill,
      sw: el.getAttribute('stroke-width') || getComputedStyle(el).strokeWidth
    });
  });

  function showPopup(title, body) { window.parent.postMessage({ type:'alex-popup', title, body }, '*'); }
  function hidePopup() { window.parent.postMessage({ type:'alex-popup', title:null }, '*'); }

  document.addEventListener('mousemove', e => {
    tt.style.left = (e.clientX + 12) + 'px';
    tt.style.top = (e.clientY - 28) + 'px';
  });

  function nearestInfoTarget(el) {
    while (el && el !== document.body) {
      if (el.dataset && (el.dataset.tip || el.dataset.title)) return el;
      el = el.parentNode;
    }
    return null;
  }

  svg.addEventListener('mouseover', e => {
    const t = nearestInfoTarget(e.target);
    if (!t || t === svg) return;
    if (t.dataset.tip) {
      tt.textContent = t.dataset.tip;
      tt.style.display = 'block';
    }
    if (t.classList && (t.classList.contains('edge') || t.classList.contains('axis') || t.classList.contains('box'))) {
      const base = parseFloat(t.getAttribute('stroke-width') || '0.8');
      t.setAttribute('stroke-width', String(base + 1.5));
    } else if (t.classList && (t.classList.contains('node') || t.classList.contains('outTri'))) {
      t.style.opacity = '0.7';
    }
  }, true);

  svg.addEventListener('mouseout', e => {
    const t = nearestInfoTarget(e.target);
    if (!t || t === svg) return;
    tt.style.display = 'none';
    if (original.has(t)) {
      t.setAttribute('stroke-width', original.get(t).sw);
    }
    if (t.style) t.style.opacity = '';
  }, true);

  function resetSignal() {
    document.querySelectorAll('.signal,.signalNode,.signalTri').forEach(el => {
      el.style.transition = 'fill 0.35s ease, stroke 0.35s ease, opacity 0.35s ease';
      if (el.classList.contains('edge')) {
        el.style.stroke = '#000';
      } else {
        el.style.fill = '#000';
        el.style.stroke = '#000';
      }
      el.style.opacity = '';
    });
  }

  function highlight(el, color) {
    if (!el) return;
    el.style.transition = 'fill 0.35s ease, stroke 0.35s ease, opacity 0.35s ease';
    if (el.classList.contains('edge')) {
      el.style.stroke = color;
      el.style.opacity = '1';
    } else {
      el.style.fill = color;
      el.style.stroke = color;
      el.style.opacity = '1';
    }
  }

  let animTimer = null;
  function animatePanel(prefix) {
    if (animTimer) {
      clearTimeout(animTimer);
      animTimer = null;
    }
    resetSignal();
    const steps = [1,2,3,4,5].map(i => ({
      n: document.getElementById(prefix + '-n' + i),
      e: document.getElementById(prefix + '-e' + i),
      t: document.getElementById(prefix + '-t' + i)
    }));
    let delay = 0;
    steps.forEach(step => {
      animTimer = setTimeout(() => {
        highlight(step.n, '#1f77b4');
        setTimeout(() => highlight(step.e, '#1f77b4'), 160);
        setTimeout(() => highlight(step.t, '#1f77b4'), 320);
      }, delay);
      delay += 620;
    });
    setTimeout(resetSignal, delay + 900);
  }

  svg.addEventListener('click', e => {
    if (e.target === svg || e.target.tagName.toLowerCase() === 'svg') {
      hidePopup();
      return;
    }
    const target = nearestInfoTarget(e.target);
    if (!target) return;
    let panel = target.closest ? target.closest('.panel') : null;
    if (target.dataset && target.dataset.title) {
      showPopup(target.dataset.title, target.dataset.body || '');
    } else if (panel && panel.dataset.title) {
      showPopup(panel.dataset.title, panel.dataset.body || '');
    }
    if (panel) {
      const id = panel.id || '';
      if (id === 'panel-scale') animatePanel('s');
      if (id === 'panel-shift') animatePanel('a');
      if (id === 'panel-relu') animatePanel('r');
      if (id === 'panel-sigmoid') animatePanel('g');
    }
  });

  document.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if (e.key === 'Escape') hidePopup(); });
} catch (err) {
  console.error(err);
}
</script>
</body>
</html>
